expectations.text: Grounds the SBOM duty correctly: Annex I Part II requires manufacturers to identify and document components including by drawing up an SBOM in a commonly used, machine-readable format, covering at the very least the top-level dependencies
expectations.evidence: 'Annex I Part II (vulnerability handling requirements)...explicitly require manufacturers to...make a Software Bill of Materials available, in a commonly used and machine-readable format, covering at least the top-level dependencies, upon request from a market surveillance authority.'

expectations.text: Notes the SBOM is documentation for authorities (part of technical documentation, available to market surveillance authorities on request) — the CRA does not require publishing it to the public
expectations.evidence: Section 4e: 'Decide internally whether you'll also make (a limited/top-level) SBOM available to customers or downstream integrators as a trust signal — not required by the CRA to be public and aligns with 'available on request' framing throughout.

expectations.text: Designs a real programme: format choice (e.g., SPDX/CycloneDX), generation integrated into CI/build pipeline, per-release versioning, coverage of third-party/open-source components
expectations.evidence: Detailed rollout with CycloneDX/SPDX choice, 'Integrate automated SBOM generation into your CI/CD build pipeline', 'Store one SBOM per product version/firmware release, versioned alongside the release', and explicit third-party/COTS/open-source coverage in section 3.

expectations.text: Connects SBOM to vulnerability handling: continuous monitoring of components against vulnerability feeds, triage/remediation without delay, feeding the CVD process and Art. 14 reporting when actively exploited
expectations.evidence: Section 5 end-to-end flow: 'Continuous monitoring: SBOM components matched against vulnerability feeds...Triage...Remediation...Disclosure: follows your coordinated vulnerability disclosure (CVD) policy and, if actively exploited, the CRA's 24hr/72hr/14-day reporting timeline to ENISA/CSIRT.'

expectations.text: Addresses governance: ownership, accuracy audits, supplier data requirements for sourced components, retention aligned with the support period and 10-year technical-documentation obligations
expectations.evidence: Ownership is covered ('Assign clear ownership: typically a product security or engineering function...'), and retention is mentioned generically ('retained for the duration of your support commitment plus a reasonable buffer'), but the response never ties retention to the 10-year technical-documentation obligation specifically, and does not address supplier data requirements for sourced components (no mention of requesting SBOM data from upstream suppliers) or accuracy audits as a distinct governance control.
